ITT ( (ITT) ) just unveiled an update.

ITT filed a brief regulatory disclosure indicating that information previously described under Item 1.01, concerning entry into a material definitive agreement, is being formally incorporated by reference into another section of its filing. The notice is purely procedural, adds no new operational or financial details, and serves mainly to align the company’s reporting structure with securities disclosure requirements.

This type of cross‑referencing is standard practice in corporate filings and is intended to ensure consistency and completeness across related disclosure items. For investors and other stakeholders, the update signals that the substantive terms of the agreement remain as previously reported, with no additional modifications or new commitments highlighted in this particular notice.

data.title: Brookdale Senior Living Reports Strong 2025 Results, Refinances Debt
data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Brookdale Senior Living ( (BKD) ) has shared an announcement.

Brookdale Senior Living reported its fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 results on February 18, 2026, showing a 5.7% increase in full-year consolidated RevPAR and a 310-basis-point improvement in fourth-quarter weighted average occupancy versus a year earlier, driven by strong move-in volume. Despite a 2025 net loss of $263 million, Adjusted EBITDA reached $458 million, above guidance, while the company reduced facility and administrative costs, lowered lease payments, and refinanced roughly $550 million of 2026–2027 mortgage debt, signaling operational momentum and a stronger capital structure that may support further growth in 2026.

The most recent analyst rating on (BKD) stock is a Buy with a $18.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Brookdale Senior Living stock, see the BKD Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on BKD Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BKD is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak profitability and a highly leveraged balance sheet with negative equity. Offsetting this, recent operational execution is improving (higher occupancy, rising adjusted EBITDA and raised guidance) and the stock’s trend is strong, though momentum appears overbought. Valuation remains unattractive due to ongoing losses and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on BKD stock, click here.

More about Brookdale Senior Living

Brookdale Senior Living Inc., based in Brentwood, Tenn., operates senior housing communities, providing assisted living, memory care, and related residential and care services. The company focuses on maximizing occupancy and revenue per available unit across its U.S. portfolio while managing operating costs and its mortgage debt profile to improve financial performance and balance sheet strength.

data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Willi Food Inte ( (WILC) ) has shared an update.

G. Willi-Food International has called a Special General Meeting of shareholders for March 24, 2026 in Yavne, Israel, according to a Form 6-K filed on February 18, 2026. Shareholders of record as of February 24, 2026 will be eligible to participate and vote, either in person or by proxy, with a quorum set at 25% of outstanding ordinary shares.

On the agenda are three key governance matters: approval of the terms of office for Mrs. Karin Williger as head of business optimization and business development effective January 1, 2026, a three-year extension of the services agreement with controlling shareholder Willi-Food Investments Ltd. from January 1, 2026, and the election of Ms. Michal Fishler Mund as an external director for a three-year term starting June 1, 2026. These votes could shape the company’s leadership structure and its relationship with its controlling shareholder, with special approval thresholds applying to related-party and external director items under Israeli corporate law.

The most recent analyst rating on (WILC) stock is a Buy with a $33.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Willi Food Inte stock, see the WILC Stock Forecast page.

Willi Food Inte’s strong financial performance and technical indicators are the primary drivers of its high score. The stock’s attractive valuation further supports its investment appeal. However, the lack of earnings call data and corporate events limits additional insights.

Above Food Ingredients ( (ABVE) ) has issued an update.

Above Food Ingredients Inc. disclosed that it received, as expected, a Nasdaq staff determination letter on February 3, 2026, stating it was not in compliance with Listing Rule 5250(c)(2) because of a delay in filing interim financial statements for the six months ended July 31, 2025. The company has completed those interim accounts and plans to submit them shortly after filing its audited Form 20-F, which it says is in the final stages, and it emphasized that the Nasdaq notice has no immediate effect while it moves to restore full listing compliance and reassure shareholders about the rigor of its reporting process.

data.description:
President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Carlyle Tactical Private Credit Fund L ( (TAKLX) ) has provided an update.

Carlyle Tactical Private Credit Fund is currently conducting a private offering of preferred stock to select institutional investors, aiming to raise additional capital to support its private credit investment activities. The fund notes that issuing preferred stock will increase leverage in its portfolio, which can magnify potential losses and entails greater effective leverage than the use of debt under the Investment Company Act of 1940, heightening risk for investors and signaling a more aggressive capital structure.

More about Carlyle Tactical Private Credit Fund L

Carlyle Tactical Private Credit Fund is an investment fund focused on private credit strategies, raising capital from institutional investors to build and manage a leveraged portfolio of credit assets. It operates under the Investment Company Act of 1940, which shapes how it can use instruments such as preferred stock and debt to enhance returns through leverage.

data.description:

The latest announcement is out from VCI Global Limited ( (VCIG) ).

On February 18, 2026, VCI Global said its blockchain arm Smart Bridge Technologies signed a definitive partnership agreement with Mezzofy Holding to build a Real-World Asset digital coupon exchange focused on tokenized vouchers. The structure gives Smart Bridge 51% control of the planned entity and regulatory and settlement leadership, while Mezzofy contributes its technology stack and multinational merchant network, aiming to plug into an existing enterprise ecosystem rather than rely on speculative retail demand.

The move marks a shift from development to commercial deployment in VCI Global’s digital asset strategy, positioning the exchange as a core infrastructure pillar expected to generate transaction fees, enterprise tokenization services and settlement revenues. By targeting the large but fragmented global digital voucher market and enabling secondary trading and price discovery for previously illiquid vouchers, the initiative could enhance VCI Global’s role as an early infrastructure provider in enterprise-focused digital commerce, though final capital commitments and the actual formation of the joint entity remain subject to further agreements and approvals.

CCFNB Bancorp ( (CCFN) ) just unveiled an update.

On February 18, 2026, Muncy Columbia Financial Corporation’s board declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.46 per share for the first quarter of 2026, payable March 19, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 4. The board also approved a special one-time cash dividend of $1.00 per share, payable April 23, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 8, continuing the company’s practice of supplementing recurring payouts with occasional special distributions.

The announcement highlighted record 2025 earnings of $24.2 million, or $6.85 per share, up from $19.0 million, or $5.33 per share, in 2024, alongside improved returns on assets and equity. Capital and credit metrics also strengthened, with book value per share rising 15.6% to $54.44, the bank’s Tier 1 leverage ratio increasing to 9.93%, and past-due loans falling to 1.11%, supporting management’s message that the institution is financially strong and well-positioned to navigate market challenges while continuing to reward shareholders.

The institution concentrates on steady growth, disciplined credit underwriting, and maintaining capital ratios above well-capitalized regulatory thresholds while serving the banking needs of its regional communities.

The company’s strategy emphasizes returning excess capital to shareholders through regular and special cash dividends, supported by strengthened earnings and balance sheet metrics. data.description:

Irsa Inversiones Y Representaciones SA ( (IRS) ) has issued an announcement.

On February 18, 2026, IRSA announced to warrant holders that the penultimate exercise window before the instruments’ May 2026 expiry has opened, running from February 17 to February 25, 2026, with intermediaries potentially setting earlier internal deadlines. Each warrant entitles the holder to receive 1.6367 common shares with a par value of ARS 10 at an exercise price of USD 0.2641 per share, providing a defined path to convert derivative exposure into equity.

Starting with this window, holders may also use a “Net Exercise with Par Value Contribution” cashless modality, approved at the October 30, 2025 shareholders’ meeting, receiving only the shares corresponding to the difference between the cash exercise price and the reference market value while paying only par value plus any applicable GDS issuance fee. This alternative structure could encourage higher warrant conversion and broaden the company’s shareholder base ahead of the May 2026 maturity, potentially supporting liquidity in both the local shares and their GDS equivalents.

data.title: Cresud Announces Final Warrant Exercise Window and Cashless Option

The latest update is out from Cresud Sociedad ( (CRESY) ).

On February 18, 2026, Cresud informed investors that the final exercise window for its share warrants will run from February 17 to February 25, 2026, with intermediaries such as Caja de Valores potentially imposing earlier internal cut-off dates for receiving exercise instructions. Each warrant entitles the holder to receive 1.5417 common shares with a par value of ARS 1 at an exercise price of USD 0.3669 per share, allowing warrant holders to finalize their position in Cresud equity.

For this last exercise period, the company is offering a “Net Exercise with Par Value Contribution” cashless alternative approved at the shareholders’ meeting on October 30, 2025, under which holders receive shares equal to the difference between the cash exercise price and the reference market value while paying only the ARS 1 par value per share. In the case of converting common shares into ADSs, investors will also pay the ADS issuance fee of USD 0.05 per ADS, a structure that could ease capital outlay for warrant holders and potentially support an increase in Cresud’s outstanding share capital and market liquidity.

The most recent analyst rating on (CRESY) stock is a Hold with a $13.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Cresud Sociedad stock, see the CRESY Stock Forecast page.

Gladstone Investment ( (GAIN) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Gladstone Investment Corporation has issued up to $115 million of 7.125% unsecured notes due May 1, 2031, under a supplemental indenture executed with UMB Bank on February 18, 2026. The notes, which closed the same day, rank pari passu with the company’s existing unsecured debt, pay interest quarterly beginning May 1, 2026, and are redeemable at par on or after May 1, 2028.

Proceeds from the offering will be used to repay a portion of borrowings under Gladstone Investment’s credit facility, fund new investment opportunities and for general corporate purposes, with the company planning to re-borrow under the facility to continue investing in portfolio companies as conditions permit. The indenture also imposes covenants tying leverage and reporting obligations to Investment Company Act standards, providing noteholders with structural protections while supporting the firm’s ongoing growth strategy in the lower middle-market financing space.

Heritage Distilling Holding Company ( (IPST) ) has shared an announcement.

On February 17, 2026, IP Strategy Holdings, Inc., formerly Heritage Distilling Holding Company, Inc., filed a Third Amended and Restated Certificate of Incorporation in Delaware to change its corporate name, effective February 18, 2026. The board simultaneously approved amended and restated bylaws to align with the new name, while the company’s common stock continued trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the unchanged IPST ticker and CUSIP.

In a February 18, 2026 press release, the company said the rebranding to IP Strategy Holdings underscores its shift toward programmable intellectual property and AI-native data networks, including its $IP token treasury and validator role in the Story network ecosystem. Nasdaq is expected to reflect the new name on its platform from February 19, 2026, signaling a formal pivot from its heritage distilling identity to a digital IP-focused strategy for public market investors.

Eni SPA ( (E) ) has issued an update.

During the week of 9–13 February 2026, Eni bought 2,220,844 of its own shares on Euronext Milan, equal to 0.07% of its share capital, at a weighted average price of €18.0112 per share, for a total outlay of about €40 million under its previously approved treasury share buyback programme. The move is part of a wider capital management strategy that has seen Eni repurchase 117.6 million shares, or 3.74% of capital, since May 2025 and lift its total treasury stock, including shares used for executive and employee plans, to 204.4 million shares, or 6.5% of the company’s share capital as of 18 February 2026.
